stories.title: Serena Williams and Mboko Win Doubles Opener at Queen's Club
stories.angle: Serena Williams and Victoria Mboko defeated No. 3 seeds Nicole Melichar-Martinez and Erin Routliffe 7-6(2), 6-2 in the first round of doubles at the HSBC Championships at Queen's Club. Williams, 44, looked sharp — dominant on serve, decisive at net. Mboko, 19, matched her partner's intensity throughout. After a tight first set decided in a tiebreak, the pair pulled away in the second. They next face Leylah Fernandez and Laura Siegemund in the quarterfinals. Williams said after the match: the result matters, but so does the experience. The comeback is real.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Serena Williams and Victoria Mboko beat the No. 3 seeds 7-6(2), 6-2.

The comeback is producing results. First match back since the 2022 US Open — and she's through to the quarterfinals.

Next up: Fernandez and Siegemund. 🎾 #QueensClub #TennisFanrecap #WTA

stories.title: Kyrgios Wins Stuttgart Return — Defeats Moutet 6-3, 6-4
stories.angle: Nick Kyrgios defeated No. 8 seed Corentin Moutet 6-3, 6-4 in 69 minutes in the first round of the BOSS Open in Stuttgart. Kyrgios fired 8 aces. Moutet didn't earn a single break point — a testament to Kyrgios' serve dominance on grass. Kyrgios: "I'm very thrilled to be back." It was his first singles win on a grass court since reaching the Wimbledon final in 2022. His next match is against Japanese qualifier Sho Shimabukuro. Also Day 2 at Stuttgart: Frances Tiafoe defeated home wildcard Daniel Altmaier in a hard-fought two-hour-26-minute match. Mpetshi Perricard completed a rain-suspended match and advanced. Fritz (defending champion) and Shelton (top seed) entered the second round.

x_posts.label: Post 2
x_posts.text:
Kyrgios def. Moutet 6-3, 6-4.

8 aces. 84% first serves in. Zero — ZERO — break points for Moutet.

That is a dominance statement on grass. His first singles win on the surface since the 2022 Wimbledon final.

"I'm very thrilled to be back." 🔥 #Stuttgart #TennisFanrecap #ATPTour

stories.title: Queen's Club WTA Day 2 — Raducanu Dominant, Boulter Battles Through
stories.angle: Day 2 at the HSBC Championships delivered two signature British moments. Emma Raducanu demolished qualifier Anna Blinkova 6-0, 6-3 in 64 minutes, losing just 6 points in the first set — her most dominant grass-court performance in years. She advances to face Sorana Cirstea. Katie Boulter completed her rain-delayed first-round match against No. 8 seed Leylah Fernandez, winning 3-6, 7-6(4), 7-5 in a gutsy three-setter. Alexandra Eala also cruised through in straight sets. Later, Raducanu and Boulter paired up in doubles and lost to Zhang Shuai and Storm Hunter 6-4, 3-6, 10-5 — but the singles results are the story of the day. A British double on a British grass court.

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text:
Emma Raducanu def. Blinkova 6-0, 6-3.

Lost SIX POINTS in the first set. In 64 minutes.

On grass. In front of a home crowd. With Wimbledon 21 days away. 🌿 #QueensClub #TennisFanrecap #WTA
x_posts.tweet_type: FOLLOW-UP (Queen's Club Day 1 covered June 9; today is Day 2 results)

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text:

stories.title: Libema Open 's-Hertogenbosch — Mannarino Ends Defending Champion's Run
stories.angle: At the Libema Open in 's-Hertogenbosch, Day 2 produced a notable result: Adrian Mannarino knocked out defending champion Gabriel Diallo 6-4, 3-6, 7-5. Diallo, who won the title in 2025, bowed out in the first round. Marin Cilic also made a winning return to the Dutch grass, rallying past Denis Shapovalov. Martin Damm earned a second-round meeting with top seed Felix Auger-Aliassime. FAA and No. 2 seed Medvedev had byes and are set to enter the draw on Day 3.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text:
Defending champion Gabriel Diallo is out in the first round at 's-Hertogenbosch.

Mannarino def. Diallo 6-4, 3-6, 7-5.

FAA and Medvedev now entering the draw. Grass tournaments have no memory. 🎾 #TennisFanrecap #ATPTour
x_posts.tweet_type: FOLLOW-UP (Hertogenbosch opened June 9; today is Day 2 results)

stories.title: Grass Season Week 1 — Mid-Week Snapshot From Three Simultaneous Tournaments
stories.angle: For the first time in the 2026 season, three simultaneous grass-court events are running: the BOSS Open in Stuttgart, the Libema Open in 's-Hertogenbosch, and the HSBC Championships at Queen's Club. Each is producing results that will shape Wimbledon seedings and confidence. Kyrgios, Tiafoe, Raducanu, and Boulter are all through to the next round. The Wimbledon fortnight begins July 1 — 21 days from today. With Zverev and Andreeva carrying clay-court momentum into the grass, the grass season is setting up as genuinely open. No clear Wimbledon favourite has emerged from the first 48 hours.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text:
Wimbledon is 21 days away.

Stuttgart, 's-Hertogenbosch, and Queen's Club are all running simultaneously.

Kyrgios through. Raducanu dominant. Boulter wins. Diallo out.

No clear favourite yet. That's what makes this week interesting. 🌿 #TennisFanrecap #Wimbledon
